5. Ask learners to begin their editing by looking at capital letters e.g. Words that need to start with capital letters such as proper names and letters beginning sentences.
6. Learners go round to help colleagues who have challenges.
7. Let learners read some of the finished work in class.
core_competencies: CP, CC, Listening, Speaking, Reading and Writing

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.2.1: Show understanding of how punctuations are used appropriately in writing
indicator_description: Use: (the comma) to write an address; (the apostrophe) in contraction
examplars:
1. Revise the use of the comma before and after "Yes" and "No" in sentences; after addressing a person, e.g. Kofi, can you help me?
2. Introduce the use of the comma to write an address, and the apostrophe in contraction in context. Provide sample sentences/texts for learners to identify the target punctuations. Introduce one punctuation at a time e.g. I live at Adu Street, Adukrom. They can't do the work.
3. Have learners practise using the comma to write an address, and the apostrophe in contraction. Give pairs of learners unpunctuated sentences/texts to punctuate.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use nouns or noun phrases to refer to quantities or units.
examplars: 1. Introduce quantities and units in context. E.g. - Please, give me a piece of paper. I have a pair of trousers

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use: proper nouns to refer to festivals; common nouns: Count/non-count; Singular; Plural (regular, irregular); plural without plural marker
examplars:
1. Proper nouns: Introduce the concept in context. e.g. Christmas is coming. The people of Ga celebrate Homowo.
2. Count and non-count nouns: Revise the concept of proper nouns and common nouns giving and eliciting several examples. (Proper nouns refer to particular nouns such as names of people, places, mountains, etc.) Provide a text and let learners identify count and non-count nouns. Have groups use the nouns identified in simple sentences. Invite groups to present their work.
3. Singular and Plural (regular, irregular) nouns: Introduce singular and plural nouns in context. E.g. I need a table. Can you give me one of these tables? This child looks sick, but those children look healthy. Have learners read the sentences and observe the underlined words. Show pictures of objects/people to help learners identify regular and irregular nouns. E. g. picture of a child and a picture of children. Guide learners to discover the difference between regular and irregular plural forms: - Regular count nouns take s or es to form their plural. E.g. table – tables, chair – chairs - Irregular count nouns are nouns that do not take s or es to form their plural. E.g. child – children; ox – oxen; man – men; goose – geese. Put learners into groups. Have groups construct sentences with given singular and plural nouns and share their work with the class.
4. Plural nouns without plural markers: Have learners discover the plural nouns without plural markers in context.
core_competencies: CC, CP, PL, Reading and Writing

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use collective nouns to refer to a group of objects and people
examplars:
1. Teach collective nouns in context.
2. Introduce a text that contains examples of collective nouns E.g. such as audience, congregation, a flock of sheep, a troop of monkeys,
3. Have learners discuss the meaning of these words in context.
4. Put them into groups to provide more examples of collective nouns and use these nouns in sentences.
5. Let the groups present their work to the class for feedback.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use abstract nouns to refer to concepts and ideas
examplars:
1. Revise concrete nouns.
2. Introduce abstract nouns as nouns or names of things that cannot be touched, seen or felt. They include ghost, beauty, holiness, mercy, etc.
3. Provide a passage with concrete and abstract nouns and let learners identify each category.
4. Have learners use given concrete and abstract nouns in sentences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Differentiate between how the simple past and the present perfect tense forms are used in speech and in writing
examplars:
1. Distribute a sample story and let learners identify the simple past verbs to observe how they are used in sentences.
2. Working in pairs, learners write their own story using the simple past.
3. Guide learners to do their own editing paying attention to the correct use of the simple past.
4. Follow the same procedure to guide learners to identify uses of the present perfect tense.
5. Have learners compare how the two tenses are different.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Use the simple past verb form to express past needs, feelings and interest
examplars:
1. Let learners write on a story. E.g. What happened after school on Monday?
2. Discus the essay topic with learners and use the webbing strategy to help them write on the chosen topic. Learners write individually.
3. Learners exchange their work and edit as teacher guides by focusing on the past verb form and past participle form..
4. Guide learners to rewrite the essay incorporating the corrections.
5. Let learners write on another topic individually using the past verb form.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Use past perfect in speech and in writing
examplars:
1. Narrate an event in the past.
2. Discuss the narration and have learners identify sentences that are in the simple past and those in the past perfect.
3. Put learners in groups to write their own narratives on a given topic making use of both simple past and past perfect sentences.
4. Have groups share their work with the class.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Use singular and plural subjects and the verb forms that go with them.
examplars:
1. Note: Let learners use the various tenses(simple, present perfect and past perfect).
2. Revise work done on subject-verb agreement and give additional practice.
3. Review the listed tenses.
4. Demonstrate that more than one of these may be used in an essay, e.g. the simple present and the simple past as well as simple past and the past perfect.
5. Show samples of texts from their reader and elsewhere.
6. Provide topics for learners to practise with their groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.5.1: Demonstrate understanding of adjectives in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use superlative forms of regular and irregular adjectives to make comparison
examplars:
1. Show a picture of a family and guide the learners to talk about the people using the positive comparative and superlative forms of adjectives.
2. In groups, learners write a description of the members in each group.
3. Let them edit their work focusing on the use of forms of adjectives appropriately.
4. Learners can add the group picture to their presentation and publish in their class magazine.
5. Learners now write individually by describing the members of their nuclear families demonstrating their knowledge of the positive, comparative and superlative forms.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.6.1: Demonstrate understanding of the use of adverbs in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use adverbs to express manner
examplars:
1. Revise verbs and adverbs of place, time and manner.
2. Provide a passage with adverbs of manner.
3. Have learners identify the adverbs and their functions – modifying verbs. E.g. Mercy ate slowly.
4. Pair up learners to write short paragraphs using adverbs of manner.
5. Have pairs present their work to the class for feedback.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.7.1: Apply the knowledge of prepositions in oral and written communication
indicator_description: Use prepositions to convey a variety of meanings
examplars:
1. Use prepositions in sentences to convey the following meanings: - Position e.g. on - Direction e.g. to - Time e.g. at - Purpose e.g. for - Possession e.g. for - Comparison e.g. taller than - Support or opposition e.g. for you, against you
2. Provide a text and guide learners with examples to identify positions used to show the following meanings.
3. Let learners construct sentences to convey the following uses of prepositions, to show: i. Position – she is standing by the car. ii. Direction – They went towards the East. iii. Time – He left at dawn. iv. Purpose – I bought this hoe for weeding my garden. v. Possession – The shirt is for Kofi vi. Comparison vii. Support or opposition – My views are against his plans.
4. Have learners write a short paragraph containing examples of the prepositions discussed.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.8.1: Apply the knowledge of conjunctions in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Identify and use simple conjunctions – because, since, so, although – to give reasons, link contrasting ideas, show results
examplars:
1. Revise simple conjunctions. Provide simple sentences and let learners join them using and, or/nor, but.
2. Introduce because, since, so, although, in context. e.g.: I'm late because I missed the bus. Although Afi is has eye problems, she doesn't wear glasses. He eats well, so he is healthy.
3. Guide learners to discover the functions of these conjunctions.
4. Have them practise using these conjunctions in their own sentences.
5. Provide a passage and have learners identify the conjunctions in the passage.
6. Let learners form sentences and join them with the conjunctions identified.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Using Simple, Compound and Complex Sentences
content_standard: B5.5.9.1: Apply knowledge of grammatical rules to form words, phrases and sentences
indicator_description: Identify subjects and predicates in compound sentences
examplars:
1. Give learners an extract from a comprehension passage.
2. Guide them to identify the simple and compound sentences.
3. Let them identify the subjects and verbs in each compound sentence.
4. Let learners write about an event they had participated in, demonstrating their understanding of subject and predicate sentences.
5. Learners edit to demonstrate their knowledge of subject and predicate.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Using Simple, Compound and Complex Sentences
content_standard: B5.5.9.1: Apply knowledge of grammatical rules to form words, phrases and sentences
indicator_description: Construct compound sentences correctly
examplars:
1. Review the use of coordinators in sentences.
2. Learners write examples of sentences containing coordinates. Let them write sentences demonstrating their knowledge in subject and predicate.
3. Learners in groups write a narrative exhibiting their knowledge of subject and predicate in compound sentences.
4. Guide them to edit their work and encourage them to share the final products with the class.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B5.5.10.1: Spell words correctly
indicator_description: Use phonics knowledge to spell words
examplars:
1. In groups, learners plan a spelling activity. Each selects a number of words. Learners create a context E.g. i. It is eaten at Christmas. ii. It is made of flour, eggs, sugar etc. iii. It is baked.
2. Learners identify the word, say it, spell and use it in a sentence. This is a writing activity that can be done among two groups.
3. The scores are recorded and the champions rewarded.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Building the Love and Culture of Reading
content_standard: B5.6.1.1: Read widely for pleasure, and to demonstrate independent reading and learning in the literary/content areas
indicator_description: Read a variety of age- and level appropriate books and present at least a-three-paragraph summary of each book read
examplars:
1. Have learners read independently books of their choice during the library period.
2. Learners think-pair-share their stories with peers.
3. Ask each learner to write a-two-three paragraph summary of the book read.
4. Invite individuals to present their work to the class for feedback.
